# Instagram Post — Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Drops to $0.25 Per Million Input Tokens — AI Inference Costs in Freefall
# Week: Week of 2026-04-06
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-04-12T23:02:45.838Z

---

AI just became cheaper than your coffee budget.

Google released Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite at $0.25 per million input tokens. For context, that is 2.5x faster response times and 45% faster output generation than previous versions — at a fraction of the price.

This is not an incremental price drop. This is cost demolition. Eighteen months ago, equivalent AI capability cost 10 to 50 times more. The deflation curve in AI compute is now steeper than cloud ever was.

What does this mean for business leaders? The cost objection for AI adoption is officially dead. When inference is nearly free, the bottleneck shifts to something harder to buy — organizational readiness. Data quality. Workflow architecture. Speed of deployment.

Companies still running AI pilots and waiting for costs to come down have run out of reasons to wait. The question is no longer whether you can afford AI. It is whether you can afford to be slow.

GYanTech AI builds systems designed for scale when compute is abundant. Link in bio.
